children.heading: Computer and network security research grants
children.label: a Computer and network security research grants
A
children.text: authentication, cryptography, and other secure data communications technology;

children.label: B
children.text: computer forensics and intrusion detection;

children.label: C
children.text: reliability of computer and network applications, middleware, operating systems, control systems, and communications infrastructure;

children.label: D
children.text: privacy and confidentiality;

children.label: E
children.text: network security architecture, including tools for security administration and analysis;

children.label: F
children.text: emerging threats;

children.label: G
children.text: vulnerability assessments and techniques for quantifying risk;

children.label: H
children.text: remote access and wireless security;

children.label: I
children.text: enhancement of law enforcement ability to detect, investigate, and prosecute cyber-crimes, including those that involve piracy of intellectual property;

children.label: J
children.text: secure fundamental protocols that are integral to inter-network communications and data exchange;

children.label: i
children.text: programming languages and systems that include fundamental security features;

children.label: ii
children.text: portable or reusable code that remains secure when deployed in various environments;

children.label: iii
children.text: verification and validation technologies to ensure that requirements and specifications have been implemented; and

children.label: iv
children.text: models for comparison and metrics to assure that required standards have been met;

children.label: i
children.text: addresses the building of secure systems from trusted and untrusted components;

children.label: ii
children.text: proactively reduces vulnerabilities;

children.label: iii
children.text: addresses insider threats; and

children.label: iv
children.text: supports privacy in conjunction with improved security;

children.label: M
children.text: monitoring and detection;

children.label: N
children.text: mitigation and rapid recovery methods;

children.label: O
children.text: security of wireless networks and mobile devices;

children.label: P
children.text: security of cloud infrastructure and services;

children.label: Q
children.text: security of election-dedicated voting system software and hardware; and

children.label: R
children.text: role of the human factor in cybersecurity and the interplay of computers and humans and the physical world.

children.heading: Merit review; competition
children.label: 2 Merit review; competition
children.text: Grants shall be awarded under this section on a merit-reviewed competitive basis.

children.label: A
children.text: $35,000,000 for fiscal year 2003;

children.label: B
children.text: $40,000,000 for fiscal year 2004;

children.label: C
children.text: $46,000,000 for fiscal year 2005;

children.label: D

children.label: E

children.heading: Computer and network security research centers
children.label: b Computer and network security research centers

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall award multiyear grants, subject to the availability of appropriations, to institutions of higher education, nonprofit research institutions, or consortia thereof to establish multidisciplinary Centers for Computer and Network Security Research. Institutions of higher education, nonprofit research institutions, or consortia thereof receiving such grants may partner with 1 or more government laboratories or for-profit institutions, or other institutions of higher education or nonprofit research institutions.

children.heading: Merit review; competition
children.label: 2 Merit review; competition
children.text: Grants shall be awarded under this subsection on a merit-reviewed competitive basis.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 3 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Centers shall be to generate innovative approaches to computer and network security by conducting cutting-edge, multidisciplinary research in computer and network security, including improving the security and resiliency of information technology, reducing cyber vulnerabilities, and anticipating and mitigating consequences of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure, by conducting research in the areas described in subsection (a)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: the research projects that will be undertaken by the Center and the contributions of each of the participating entities;

children.label: B
children.text: how the Center will promote active collaboration among scientists and engineers from different disciplines, such as computer scientists, engineers, mathematicians, and social science researchers;

children.label: C
children.text: how the Center will contribute to increasing the number and quality of computer and network security researchers and other professionals, including individuals from groups historically underrepresented in these fields; and

children.label: D
children.text: how the Center will disseminate research results quickly and widely to improve cyber security in information technology networks, products, and services.

children.label: A
children.text: the ability of the applicant to generate innovative approaches to computer and network security and effectively carry out the research program;

children.label: B
children.text: the experience of the applicant in conducting research on computer and network security and the capacity of the applicant to foster new multidisciplinary collaborations;

children.label: C
children.text: the capacity of the applicant to attract and provide adequate support for a diverse group of undergraduate and graduate students and postdoctoral fellows to pursue computer and network security research;

children.label: D
children.text: the extent to which the applicant will partner with government laboratories, for-profit entities, other institutions of higher education, or nonprofit research institutions, and the role the partners will play in the research undertaken by the Center;

children.label: E
children.text: the demonstrated capability of the applicant to conduct high performance computation integral to complex computer and network security research, through on-site or off-site computing;

children.label: F
children.text: the applicant’s affiliation with private sector entities involved with industrial research described in subsection (a)(1);

children.label: G
children.text: the capability of the applicant to conduct research in a secure environment;

children.label: H
children.text: the applicant’s affiliation with existing research programs of the Federal Government;

children.label: I
children.text: the applicant’s experience managing public-private partnerships to transition new technologies into a commercial setting or the government user community;

children.label: J
children.text: the capability of the applicant to conduct interdisciplinary cybersecurity research, basic and applied, such as in law, economics, or behavioral sciences; and

children.label: K
children.text: the capability of the applicant to conduct research in areas such as systems security, wireless security, networking and protocols, formal methods and networking and information technology, nanotechnology, or industrial control systems.

children.heading: Annual meeting
children.label: 6 Annual meeting
children.text: The Director shall convene an annual meeting of the Centers in order to foster collaboration and communication between Center participants.

children.label: A
children.text: $12,000,000 for fiscal year 2003;

children.label: B
children.text: $24,000,000 for fiscal year 2004;

children.label: C
children.text: $36,000,000 for fiscal year 2005;

children.label: D
children.text: $36,000,000 for fiscal year 2006; and

children.label: E
children.text: $36,000,000 for fiscal year 2007.
